message:
test(fcp-conformance): real cross-zone enforcement suite (br-crr6l)

The retired shadow `fcp_conformance::interop::cross_zone` module used
local `CrossZoneRequest` / `ApprovalToken` / `ReasonCode` types and a
bespoke `evaluate_cross_zone_access` implementation, so the
conformance lane passed regardless of real enforcement regressions in
fcp-core. flywheel_connectors-1vcqs removed the shadow assertion;
this commit ships the replacement.

## New test binary

crates/fcp-conformance/tests/cross_zone_enforcement_conformance.rs

8 tests driving the REAL
`fcp_core::policy::PolicyEngine::evaluate_invoke` path and binding
against the crate-public types (PolicyDecisionInput, ApprovalToken,
ProvenanceRecord, DecisionReasonCode, ZonePolicyObject,
DeclassificationScope, ElevationScope, IntegrityLevel,
ConfidentialityLevel).

Coverage matrix:

  Test                                                       Expected reason
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ──────────────────────────────────
  same-zone safe invoke                                      Allow
  Work -> Community, no token                                ApprovalMissingDeclassification
  Work -> Community, Elevation-scoped token (wrong scope)    ApprovalMissingDeclassification
  Work -> Community, expired Declassification token          ApprovalMissingDeclassification
  Work -> Community, token.zone_id != input.zone_id          ApprovalMissingDeclassification
  Work -> Community, valid matching Declassification token   Allow (evidence populated)
  Community -> Work, no token                                ApprovalMissingElevation
  Community -> Work, Declassification-scoped token (wrong)   ApprovalMissingElevation

## Scope note

`DecisionReasonCode::{ApprovalExpired, ApprovalZoneMismatch}` are
defined in the policy surface but NOT emitted from evaluate_invoke
for the cross-zone flow — they are filter outputs inside
apply_declassification / apply_elevation that silently drop the
token and fall through to the generic "missing approval" code.
Tests #4 and #5 explicitly pin that fall-through so a future change
that wants to surface the distinct codes can't silently flip the
observable reason without updating this regression.

## Implementation

Chose option (b) from the bead: reimplement PolicyEngine fixtures
locally (`test_header`, `minimal_zone_policy`, `engine_for`,
`cross_zone_input`, `declassification_token`). No cross-crate API
change; `fcp-core`'s own `#[cfg(test)]`-gated helpers stay private.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-conformance --test cross_zone_enforcement_conformance
  -> 8 passed; 0 failed.

Closes flywheel_connectors-crr6l.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
